source: GKM Clinical Pharmacy — compiled from Stockley's Drug Interactions & FDA labels

interactions.mechanism: Grapefruit furanocoumarins irreversibly inhibit CYP3A4 in intestinal wall, reducing first-pass metabolism of Atorvastatin by up to 260%
interactions.effect: Plasma Atorvastatin levels surge 260% — risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis
interactions.action: AVOID COMPLETELY — no safe quantity. Even small amounts cause significant interaction
interactions.source: FDA 2012 Drug Safety Communication; Stockley's Drug Interactions 11th Ed

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitors block angiotensin II-mediated aldosterone release, reducing renal K+ excretion. Combined with dietary K+ load → severe hyperkalaemia
interactions.effect: Serum K+ > 6.0 mEq/L — potentially fatal cardiac arrhythmia (peaked T-waves, VF)
interactions.action: RESTRICT high-potassium foods. Monitor serum K+ weekly. Target dietary K+ < 2000mg/day
interactions.onset: Days to weeks of chronic co-ingestion

interactions.mechanism: Furosemide inhibits Na+/K+/2Cl- cotransporter in loop of Henle. High sodium intake overwhelms diuretic capacity, causing sodium and fluid retention
interactions.effect: Diuretic failure — oedema, hypertension, risk of pulmonary congestion in renal patients
interactions.action: STRICT sodium restriction < 1500mg/day mandatory with loop diuretics
interactions.source: BNF 2024; KDIGO 2023 — CKD Fluid & Sodium Management

interactions.mechanism: Metformin reduces hepatic gluconeogenesis and improves insulin sensitivity. High-GI foods cause rapid glucose spikes that overwhelm Metformin's glucose-lowering capacity
interactions.effect: Postprandial glucose spike 2-4 mmol/L above target despite medication — sustained HbA1c elevation
interactions.action: SUBSTITUTE with low-GI alternatives (Brown Rice, Ragi, Barley). Always consume with meals
interactions.source: ADA Standards of Care 2024; UKPDS dietary analysis

interactions.mechanism: Sulphonylureas stimulate pancreatic beta-cells to release insulin regardless of glucose level. High coconut fat content may potentiate insulin secretion via GLP-1 pathway → additive hypoglycaemia
interactions.effect: Risk of hypoglycaemia — serum glucose < 3.9 mmol/L (sweating, tremors, seizures if severe)
interactions.action: MONITOR blood glucose carefully. Limit coconut-based foods to < 100ml/meal. Educate patient on hypoglycaemia symptoms
interactions.source: Endocrine Society 2023; Case series — sulphonylurea + MCT interaction

interactions.mechanism: Polyphenols/tannins in tea chelate omeprazole, reducing bioavailability by 15-20% via pH-dependent absorption interference in acidic milieu
interactions.effect: Reduced PPI efficacy — suboptimal gastric acid suppression
interactions.action: SEPARATE administration — take Omeprazole 30-60 min before tea/coffee. No clinical dose adjustment needed
interactions.source: Stockley's Drug Interactions; European Journal of Drug Metabolism 2019

interactions.mechanism: Calcium carbonate acts as phosphate binder in GI tract when taken with meals. High dietary phosphorus overwhelms binding capacity → unbound phosphorus absorbed, worsening hyperphosphataemia
interactions.effect: Persistent hyperphosphataemia — secondary hyperparathyroidism, vascular calcification, bone disease in CKD
interactions.action: RESTRICT dietary phosphorus < 800mg/day. Take Calcium Carbonate WITH every meal for maximum binding
interactions.source: KDOQI 2020 — Mineral Metabolism Management in CKD

interactions.mechanism: Sodium Bicarbonate itself contributes to sodium load. Combined with dietary sodium → volume expansion, hypertension, and oedema in already fluid-overloaded CKD patients
interactions.effect: Sodium overload — worsening hypertension, oedema, and risk of pulmonary congestion
interactions.action: Strict low-sodium diet essential when on NaHCO3 therapy. Total dietary + medicinal Na must stay < 2000mg/day
interactions.source: KDIGO 2023; Renal Association UK Dietary Guidelines

interactions.mechanism: High dietary fibre binds Tramadol in GI tract, reducing absorption rate. Effect is modest but clinically relevant when post-surgical pain control is critical
interactions.effect: Delayed Tramadol peak concentration — potentially inadequate post-operative pain control in 24-48h window
interactions.action: AVOID high-fibre meals within 2 hours of Tramadol dose. Low-fibre diet appropriate in context of post-GI surgery
interactions.onset: Affects absorption kinetics within 2 hours of co-ingestion
interactions.source: Stockley's Drug Interactions; Palliative Pharmacology Review 2022

interactions.mechanism: Metoclopramide accelerates gastric emptying via D2 antagonism. High-fat meals delay gastric emptying, opposing prokinetic action
interactions.effect: Reduced prokinetic efficacy — gastroparesis persists, nausea not adequately controlled post-surgery
interactions.action: AVOID high-fat meals. Maintain low-fat diet consistent with post-surgical nutrition protocol

interactions.mechanism: Soluble fibre may bind bile acid resins and slightly reduce statin absorption when co-administered. Effect is small but additive to other statin metabolism interactions
interactions.effect: Minor reduction (5-10%) in Atorvastatin AUC — generally clinically insignificant at therapeutic doses
interactions.action: SEPARATE statin from high-fibre foods by 1 hour. No dose adjustment needed
interactions.onset: Only when fibre ingested simultaneously with statin
interactions.source: Stockley's Drug Interactions — Statins & Dietary Fibre section

interactions.mechanism: High sodium intake activates RAAS, counteracting ACEi antihypertensive effect. Each 100mmol increase in sodium blunts ACEi response by 30-40%
interactions.effect: Reduced antihypertensive efficacy — BP target not achieved despite medication
interactions.action: Low-sodium diet essential for optimal ACEi effect. Target < 2000mg/day
interactions.source: NICE Hypertension Guidelines 2023; ONTARGET trial dietary sub-analysis

interactions.mechanism: Sulphonylureas already maximally stimulate insulin. High-GI carbs cause glucose surges requiring more insulin, eventually depleting beta-cell reserve faster. Risk of hypoglycaemia if meal delayed after dose
interactions.effect: Unpredictable glycaemia — either hypoglycaemia (dose without meal) or inadequate control (high-GI meal)
interactions.action: ALWAYS eat immediately after Glipizide dose. Choose low-GI carbohydrates for consistent glucose control

interactions.mechanism: High-fat meals trigger CCK release and increase vagal afferent activity, potentially triggering nausea that opposes Ondansetron's 5HT3 antiemetic effect
interactions.effect: Breakthrough nausea despite antiemetic — particularly relevant in post-operative nausea management
interactions.action: Maintain low-fat diet for optimal Ondansetron efficacy in post-surgical period

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol inhibits lactate oxidation in the liver, promoting lactate accumulation. Combined with Metformin (which mildly increases lactate) → risk of lactic acidosis
interactions.effect: Life-threatening lactic acidosis — serum lactate > 5 mmol/L, severe metabolic acidosis
interactions.action: ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION — alcohol completely prohibited with Metformin
interactions.source: BNF 2024; FDA Black Box Warning on Metformin + Alcohol

interactions.mechanism: Furosemide causes renal potassium wasting (hypokalaemia risk). High K+ diet may be desirable to compensate, or may be harmful depending on renal function. In CKD, K+ restriction takes priority
interactions.effect: Complex interaction — monitor serum K+ closely. CKD patients need K+ restriction despite furosemide-induced wasting
interactions.action: Check serum K+ weekly. If K+ < 3.5 mEq/L, modest K+ supplementation indicated. If K+ > 5.0 mEq/L (CKD), restrict dietary K+

interactions.mechanism: ESA therapy increases RBC production, elevating iron and phosphorus demand. Dietary phosphorus load worsens baseline hyperphosphataemia common in CKD-anaemia patients on dialysis
interactions.effect: Compounding hyperphosphataemia — independent of ESA mechanism but clinically relevant in monitoring
interactions.action: Maintain phosphate restriction as per renal diet guidelines. No dose adjustment needed for ESA
interactions.source: NKF KDOQI 2020 — Anaemia Management in CKD
